{"query":"Pope Leo XIV France apostolic visit September 2026 itinerary schedule cities","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1663,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://parisjetaime.com/eng/event/pope-s-visit-to-paris-e2356","title":"Pope Leo XIV's visit to Paris in September 2026","content":"## Pope Leo XIV will visit Paris on September 25 and 26, 2026, as part of his trip to France
This apostolic journey, the first by a pontiff to the capital since 2008, will feature several highlights.
Notre-Dame de Paris cathedral has reopened!"},{"url":"https://www.lourdes-france.com/en/pope-at-lourdes/","title":"Pope at Lourdes","content":"As part of his apostolic journey to France, he will become a pilgrim among pilgrims.
As a pilgrim, the Pope will visit the Grotto of Massabielle on Sunday 27th September.
In the afternoon, he will preside over an open-air Mass, open to all, on the large prairie in the Sanctuary, and will take part in the Marian torchlight procession.
The Sanctuary has previously had the honour of welcoming Saint John Paul II and Benedict XVI.
The improvements made to the visitor routes, the services provided, accessibility and the infrastructure will benefit the millions of pilgrims who visit Lourdes every year for many years to come.
Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, Friday: 9:30am-12 noon / 2pm-5pm"},{"url":"https://www.via.bible/en/visit-leon-france-2026/","title":"Visit of Leo XIV to France 2026","content":"Discover why Leo XIV's visit to France, between AI, human dignity and Lourdes, marked a turning point for the Church and society.
📖 Leo XIV in France: why this apostolic journey is a grace for the Church and for the Republic Pope Leo XIV expected in France from September 25th to 28th Diocese of Paris
The apostolic visit of Leo XIV to France in September 2026 is an unprecedented event since John Paul II.
For a pope whose first magisterial act — Magnifica Humanitas — focused on human dignity in the digital age, choosing France as the first major European trip was not a coincidence.
As such, it follows in the tradition of the foundational visits of John Paul II and Benedict XVI — each time, a Church that comes to remind a secularized nation that the question of man remains open.
Three places structure this journey: Paris, Lyon and Lourdes.
Paris for dialogue with culture and power; ; Lyon for the rootedness in the tradition of the martyrs and the living memory of the Church of France; ; Lourdes to remind us that the Gospel is addressed first and foremost to the poor and the suffering — those whom algorithms do not optimize.
For the Pope of Magnifica Humanitas — who concludes his encyclical on Mary as a model of a humanity that welcomes without dissolving — Lourdes is a theological coherence.
Here you will find articles published on VIA.bible about this apostolic visit: analyses of the speeches, reactions from the Church in France, and contextualization with Magnifica Humanitas and Catholic social tradition."},{"url":"https://www.ombredelatour.fr/blog/pope-leo-xivs-visit-to-lourdes-programme-and-dates-27-september-2026","title":"Pope Leo XIV's Visit to Lourdes: Programme and Dates (27 ...","content":"The dates: a trip to France from 25 to 28 September 2026 · 25 and 26 September: Paris · 27 September: Lourdes · 28 September: Metz.Read more"}]}
